attributes.name: Emily
attributes.patient_agreement: agrees with the plan for monthly Lucentis injections ("that sounds great")
attributes.notes: Still smoking about a pack of cigarettes per day; has tried to stop but finds it hard. Checks blood pressure when she can remember.
Associated with straight lines/door frames appearing crooked.

attributes.impact: straight lines and door frames look crooked

attributes.notes: Reports visual distortion: straight lines and door frames appear crooked; associated with decreased vision when reading and blurring of vision.

attributes.activity_context: when taking husband out to a nice restaurant

attributes.notes: Mentioned in context of sudden visual distortion and blurring of vision with decreased vision when reading and straight lines/door frames appearing crooked.
Patient reports decreased vision when reading.

attributes.activity_amount: a pack of cigarettes a day
attributes.notes: Tried to stop every now and then but has found it hard to totally stop.
Patient reports taking blood pressure when they can remember; clinician recommends checking regularly and keeping a log.
Clinician asked whether patient is keeping up with blood pressure medication; no specific medication name or regimen stated.

attributes.procedure_type: eye examination (anterior segment, fundus, and posterior segment exam)
attributes.notes: Exam included anterior segment exam, fundus exam, and posterior segment exam.
Anterior segment examination reveals posterior chamber intraocular lenses in both eyes.

Anterior segment: posterior chamber intraocular lens; cornea clear; no MVI; cup-to-disc 0.2. Fundus: retina attached 360 degrees without tears/holes/PE changes in fovea. Posterior segment: drusen in macula; retinal thickening; subretinal fluid; exudates; positive for hemorrhage.
Right eye cup-to-disc is 0.2; left eye cup-to-disc is 0.3.
Cornea clear; no MVI; cup-to-disc 0.3; no PE changes in fovea; no hemorrhage or subretinal fluid; retina attached.
Doctor reviewed diagnostic tests including OCT and fluorescein angiography; findings consistent with neovascular age-related macular degeneration in the right eye.

attributes.result_value: drusen deposit; subretinal fluid accumulation; choroidal neovascular membrane can be visualized
attributes.notes: OCT shows drusen deposit in/at the retinal pigment epithelium with subretinal fluid accumulation; choroidal neovascular membrane visualized.

attributes.result_value: focal areas of leakage in the right eye
attributes.interpretation: consistent with neurovascular age related macular degeneration
attributes.notes: Doctor reviewed diagnostic test results; fluorescein angiography revealed focal leakage in the right eye.

attributes.impact: usually leads to more severe vision loss; can create a large blind spot in the center of the visual field
attributes.notes: Described as wet AMD; due to abnormal blood vessels growing beneath the retina that leak fluid and blood.

attributes.course: may also progress despite lucentes treatment
attributes.notes: Discussed as vision loss/vision decline in the context of neovascular age-related macular degeneration.
Abnormal blood vessels described as growing beneath the retina and leaking fluid and blood.
Wet AMD can create a large blind spot in the center of the visual field.

attributes.procedure_duration: once a month; given regularly over a period of time
attributes.notes: Vitreous portion of the eye is numbed prior to injection.
Injected into the vitreous portion of the eye after the eye has been numbed; injections given regularly over a period of time.
